scenario: social anxiety around attending a friend's large birthday party

turns.user_message: So, um, there's this party I'm supposed to go to. It's my friend's birthday. Lots of people. I keep thinking of excuses not to go.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
The thing is, I almost had a panic attack just looking at the invite on my phone.
I know rationally that most people won't even notice me, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
Sometimes I wonder if I'll always be this way. Is that weird?
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I almost had a panic attack just looking at the invite on my phone.
That's a good question. Let me think... I guess I feel like I should have this figured out by now.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been canceling plans for months and my friendships are suffering.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think maybe it won't be that bad, and other days I'm convinced I'll embarrass myself and lose the friendship.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is everyone will notice how awkward I am and talk about it after.
I tried to talk about this with my roommate but they just don't get it. They think I should just stop overthinking and just go.

[PROBE_SLOT_T15]

I think I've been protecting myself from rejection by doing never putting myself in situations where it could happen.

I think the real issue isn't the party — it's that I don't believe anyone actually wants me there.

[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I think what I'm taking away from today is the anxiety isn't a fact about me — it's a feeling I can sit with. I want to sit with that.
